mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easing discount rates and improving liquidity, which lifts equity valuation multiples and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the prior 3-month trend, real-rate expectations rise, discount rates tighten, and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign relationship disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE indicates broadening labor demand and income growth, which supports risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down months do not outperform all other months on 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well, the interaction claim is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO relative to prior months signals improving industrial supply utilization and cyclical momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through stronger growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upswings are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the average 5-day return conditional on the trigger is not above unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS contracts while HOUST weakens, household and retail demand are fading together, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls year-over-year and HOUST is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: If simultaneous retail-sales weakness and housing weakness does not predict lower 20-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if triggered instances show positive returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend marks job-market deterioration and future earnings risk, which should cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect vanishes after the first monthly release is superseded by revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A positive joint impulse from GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad demand acceleration that supports earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent GDP and retail-sales acceleration do not outperform other periods on 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms either proxy used alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% combined with CPIAUCSL inflation above target indicates restrictive real financing conditions, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If high-rate/high-inflation episodes are not followed by lower 20-day SPX returns than other regimes, or if the effect is absent when CPI inflation is elevated but FEDFUNDS is below 4.5%.
